President +. Z. Goodall Of the
chamber of commerce has returned
from Seattle. where he attended the
arrival Of the Japanese commercial
visitors as q member of the asso'
ciated Pacific coast chambers Of
commerce that have united to en-
terrain the visitors. The visitors
will arrive In Spokane over the s..
p. q 8. at $3.50 a. m. Saturday
September II.


The new we'll at the Isolation
hospital has been set in operation
The pump is operated by electricity
and flows 1900 gallons per hour.
City freight and passenger Of
fees will close at 1 p. m. Monday
Labor day. The freight stations
will close at noon.


The school board In 4 joint ses
sion with the board of health voted
to employ five medical inspectors
for the schools so that an inspec
ton of the pupils may be made or
| the reopening of school next week
Applications for these places are
now being received by DR.. M. B
Grieve, city health officer. One
chief examiner will get SIS pei
month and the other four will re
ceive $50 each.


Madame Brew, the snake charm
er who was bitten by pet rattle
snake Wednesday, has so far re
covered as to proceed with her hus
band to Ogden, Utah. An amputa
ton of the arm will not be neces
sary.


Two diamond rings, valued Al
$3.50. were lost yesterday by Mrs
Louise De Lion of the Villa Nova
apartment house while shopping or
Riverside avenue.


The Rev. Louis Brown Of Val
paraiso, Ind. will take charge OF
the Dean Avenue Christian church
Tomorrow.


The federal authorities are still
at loss as to what to do in the
case of James Caprio, desertel
from the navy. who has been try
ing to have himself placed under
arrest for a week. No one seems
to have the authority to place
Caprio in custody.


The Spokane County Good Roads
association will assist the county
commissioners in expanding the In
creased appropriation for good
roads made by the last legislature

ice Justice Mann on charge Of

promise to have one of the largest
thi~ken exhibits ever seen In the

lair.
